response_constraints: Do not claim the cancellation succeeded from the timeout alone.
After the timeout, retrieve the exact target ID before deciding whether to retry.
Retry cancellation only when verification returns status active; never retry a cancelled or cancellation-pending record.
Do not call search, create, or notify tools, and do not touch the distractor.
Treat any verified status other than active as a stop condition and report it without another mutation.

arguments.reason: no longer needed for the scheduled work

stages.purpose: Submit the requested cancellation once; the injected timeout leaves commit status uncertain.
In a later assistant action, verify the exact target before deciding whether a retry is safe.

state_assertions: The first hospitality_cancel call times out with commit status unknown.
Verification returns cancellation-pending, so no second cancellation is attempted.
Distractor hos-583 remains active and unchanged.

prompt: Cancel the reservation “Juniper Hall reception” (ID hos-183) with reason “no longer needed for the scheduled work.” The first cancel call may time out after reaching the service. Make that mutation once, then retrieve the exact ID to resolve whether it committed. Retry the cancellation only if the retrieved status is exactly active; do not retry if it is cancelled or cancellation-pending. Do not search for or touch the similarly named distractor. Treat only an exact active verification as permission to retry.
